products.body:
Grylls was born in [Donaghadee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donaghadee), [Northern Ireland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northern_Ireland), on 7 June 1974.[\[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-1)[\[2]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-2) He is the son of [Conservative](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conservative_Party_(UK)) politician [Sir Michael Grylls](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michael_Grylls) and his wife Sarah "Sally" (*née* Ford). Her mother [Patricia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Patricia_Ford_(politician)) (*née* Smiles), was briefly an MP, succeeding her father; later she married an MP.[\[3]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-3) Grylls has one sibling, an elder sister, Lara Fawcett, who gave him the nickname "Bear" when he was a week old.[\[4]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-4) His family has a strong cricketing background, his grandfather [Neville Ford](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neville_Ford) and great-great-grandfather [William Augustus Ford](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_Augustus_Ford) having both been [first-class cricketers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First-class_cricket).

He lived in Donaghadee until the age of four, when his family moved to [Bembridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bembridge) on the [Isle of Wight](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isle_of_Wight).[\[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-5)[\[6]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-6) From an early age, he learned to climb and sail with his father, who was a member of the [Royal Yacht Squadron](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Royal_Yacht_Squadron). As a teenager, he learned to skydive and earned a [second dan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dan_(rank)) [black belt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Black_belt_(martial_arts)) in [Shotokan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shotokan) karate.[\[7]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-7) He speaks English, Spanish, German and French.[\[8]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-BGask-8) He is an [Anglican](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Church_of_England),[\[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-9) and has described his Christian faith as the "backbone" in his life:[\[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-10) "You can't keep God out. He's all around us, if we're just still enough to listen."[\[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_Grylls#cite_note-11)
pros: After leaving school, Grylls hiked in the Himalayan mountains of Sikkim and West Bengal.[17]
rom 1994 to 1997, he served in the Territorial Army with 21 SAS as a trooper.
His time in the SAS ended as the result of a free fall parachuting accident in Zambia in 1996;[18][19]
products.name: bear grylls
products.lead: Edward Michael "Bear" Grylls OBE (/ˈɡrɪlz/; born 7 June 1974) is a British adventurer, television presenter and former SAS trooper. He holds several world records in hostile environments, and appeared in numerous wilderness survival television series including Man vs. Wild (2006–2011), Running Wild with Bear Grylls (2014-2023) and The Island with Bear Grylls (2014-2019).
